mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior three months, discount rates decline and equity multiples expand, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts are followed by persistently negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If triggered easing windows do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the average 20-day return is negative across any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which pressures corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should appear after unemployment crosses a deterioration threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold events are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not negative in backtests spanning any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while CPIAUCSL remains stable, nominal consumer spending is improving without an offsetting inflation squeeze, supporting near-term earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction signal does not outperform either single proxy alone, or if positive 5-day returns occur in less than 55% of periods where RSAFS rises and CPIAUCSL is flat-to-down over a 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and capacity utilization, which raises cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The claim fails if industrial strength does not translate into later equity gains.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after 3-month rises in INDPRO, or if the sign is negative in more than one-third of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in HOUST below its recent baseline signals housing demand deterioration and tighter credit conditions, which spill into broader risk assets and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% versus 6 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drawdowns are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or if the conditional mean return is not negative over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still rising faster than the prior 3-month pace, real-rate pressure and inflation persistence jointly compress equity valuations and create negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation condition is not associated with negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample rank correlation versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, PAYEMS growth accompanied by a flat or falling UNRATE confirms broad labor-market healing, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger consumption and earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous payroll gains and stable-to-lower unemployment do not correspond to positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the signal loses predictive power after recoveries are established.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals broad demand contraction that should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as revenue growth and risk appetite weaken together.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines versus the prior quarter and RSAFS is below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent GDP and retail-sales weakness is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of triggered windows, or if the conditional average return is not negative across any 4-quarter rolling period.
